reasoning: Potassium-N-(4-toluenesulfonyl)-4-toluenesulfonamide is the potassium salt of a bis(4-toluenesulfonyl)amine (saccharin-related disulfonamide), containing two p-toluenesulfonyl groups linked via a nitrogen, making it an organosulfur compound. As a potassium salt of a sulfonamide anion, the most fitting direct parent is organic sulfate salts (organic sulfonyl-derived salt), though N,N-disubstituted sulfonamide potassium salt fits under organic sulfur salt framework.
